_meta.description: Phase 4 v12 testnet deploy artifacts — SECURITY AUDIT REMEDIATION (D-085/D-086/D-087). FRESH republish of v11 (not a compatible upgrade): the audit fixes TIGHTEN validation (D-085 fixed 32-byte seal_id + ESealIdWrongLength; D-086 mint_tokens quilt write-once EQuiltAlreadySet; D-087 policy whitelist EInvalidPolicy + ESelfRegistrationNotAllowed + ECreatorCannotSelfPurchase). These are mechanically upgrade-compatible (new private consts + asserts in existing fn bodies, no struct/signature change), but per contracts/UPGRADE.md v7/D-040 a DENY-tightening change MUST be a fresh republish — a compatible `sui client upgrade` leaves the prior package version permanently callable, so a hand-crafted PTB against the old id would bypass the new asserts entirely. Fresh republish abandons v11 state (consistent with v3–v11 precedent; no migrated demo state worth preserving — testnet PackageIDs disposable until the 8/27 mainnet ceremony). v12 re-bootstrapped a fresh TransferPolicy<NftToken> (royalty rule only, D-036 carry-forward; RuleKey verified at bootstrap digest 3S71jY8U…). SealIdRegistry freshly created in `init`. VERSION still 2 (seal gate semantics unchanged; only the seal_id length invariant tightened). === Below is the retained v10 historical note === Phase 4 v10 testnet deploy artifacts (plan-027 — paid-access entitlement split; D-078). FRESH republish (not a compatible upgrade): adds `access_fee` to LicenseTerms + a `buyers` Table to the Model3D `key` struct (layout-breaking), adds `purchase_access`/`AccessEntitlement`/`AccessPurchased`/`launch_collection_with_entitlement`, deletes `seal_approve_cap` and replaces it with `seal_approve_entitlement` (decrypt gate cap→entitlement), bumps `VERSION 1→2`, and flips the publish gate ALLOW_LIST⇒access_fee>0. Each is independently layout-/ABI-breaking → fresh original-id, abandoning v9 state (consistent with the v3–v9 precedent). v10 re-bootstrapped a fresh TransferPolicy<NftToken> (royalty rule only). === Below is the retained v9 historical note === Phase 4 v9 (plan-026 — Seal content protection; D-074/D-075/D-076). FRESH republish (not a compatible upgrade): adds `sealed_key`/`seal_id`/`preview_blob_ids`/`seal_version` fields to the Model3D `key` struct, edits the ModelPublished event layout (adds is_encrypted + preview_blob_ids), drops the `is_encrypted` param from `publish`/`new_model` (now DERIVED from policy), and changes entry-fn signatures (new `publish_encrypted`, `mint_tokens`; amended launch gate). Each is independently layout-/ABI-breaking → fresh original-id, abandoning v8 state (consistent with the v3–v8 precedent; no migrated frontend or demo pre-bake depends on v8 objects). New surface: `SealIdRegistry` (bootstrapped + shared in `init`, enforces global seal_id uniqueness — Resolution G), `seal_approve_cap`/`seal_approve_creator` (Seal key-server dry-run gate), `validate_seal_publish` (ALLOW_LIST⇒fee>0). v9 re-bootstrapped a fresh TransferPolicy<NftToken> (royalty rule only, D-036 carry-forward). Per D-009: testnet is the 6/21 submission target; mainnet ceremony deferred to the 8/27 prize window.
_meta.package_version: v12 (Phase 4 — security audit remediation D-085/D-086/D-087; fresh republish of v11, single package id, VERSION still 2). Publish digest 9gzrkk2s…, bootstrap digest 3S71jY8U…, deployer 0x3116881c… (unchanged).
_meta.supersedes_v11_package_id: 0x1cf8aa4d81788469a5ccfe8f6e119872c2afa7840b02f76013273421c90b3b6a (v11 + upgrade_cap 0x1a480d47…, publisher 0x4fd038cd…, SealIdRegistry 0xb303ecb4…, TransferPolicy 0x2e35d5bf… + cap 0xa0040ab6…). Abandoned at v12 (fresh republish for the audit DENY-tightening; old id stays callable so it must not be the active package — see _meta.description + UPGRADE.md v7/D-040).
_meta.supersedes_v10_package_id: 0x01baf4fc457047d6ae6d818063feca20038eb2d878ecae7ec9b0d1dd259cd065 (v10 + publisher 0xa01e054f…, SealIdRegistry 0x051c7ec1…, TransferPolicy 0xd151395b…). Abandoned at v11 (fresh republish; same caveat as v9→v10).
_meta.transfer_policy_rules: royalty_rule ONLY (D-036 carry-forward). Verified at the v9 bootstrap: the bootstrap tx created exactly one RuleKey dynamic field (id 0x4569a35e…) keyed by the royalty rule TypeName under kiosk_apps_package_id 0xe308bb3e….
_meta.seal_id_registry: Shared SealIdRegistry bootstrapped in `init` (D-075 Resolution G). `publish_encrypted` asserts a new model's seal_id is absent here before recording it, guaranteeing the global uniqueness that backs the is_prefix(model.seal_id, id) binding in seal_approve. Frontend reads `seal_id_registry_id` for the encrypted-publish PTB.
_meta.seal_key_servers_testnet: Mysten Open-mode 2-of-3 (threshold 2): 0x73d05d62…356db75, 0xf5d14a81…591623c8 (hardcoded in frontend/src/seal/sealClient.ts). Mainnet needs a contracted provider (deferred).
_meta.supersedes_v9_package_id: 0xba1e84ba2889b540defc11245955d3c6650a99f5251e5ee4faf69dc98a876c5c (v9 + upgrade_cap 0xfbda72ec…, publisher 0x863582ff…, SealIdRegistry 0xdb6e97f7…, TransferPolicy 0x81850ced…). Abandoned at v10 (VERSION 1→2 tripwire fails-closed any v9 encrypted model; no migration — D-078/R12).
_meta.kiosk_apps_package_id_discovery: Unchanged from v2–v8 (deps pinned identically — apps@7a07937). The deployed TransferPolicy<NftToken> royalty rule TypeName lives under 0xe308bb3e…. Frontend MUST use the address pinned here, NOT the @mysten/kiosk SDK defaults — otherwise confirm_request fails the rule membership check.
